threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and don’t bat an eye when it costs over five quid. Proper locals know the price of coffee can breach the budget.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with an off-key version of 'Wonderwall'—turns out we all knew the words.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of grilled kebabs mixing with fresh pastries at 3 AM after a night out. That’s when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop open at 3 AM, serving doner with a side of techno beats. It’s a rite of passage for night owls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can name three taco trucks by smell alone, especially when the BART's delayed and you’re hungry.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni this morning with a guy loudly explaining how the fare evasion fines have their own 'economy.' It’s a wild world down here.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for sipping vermut, but locals know it’s all about the evening petanque games that spill into the streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell new arrivals by how they stare at the public transport maps like they’re solving a Rubik's cube, totally lost but determined.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube' instead of using 'the Overground' for anything north of the river. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught a bloke singing Frank Ocean on the 63 last night—thought it was a bus karaoke night! Only in Peckham do people truly let loose after dark.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a stray cat napping on my balcony, and it didn’t budge when I stepped outside—just another Neukölln resident, I guess.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice the random techno parties in abandoned buildings? Only in Berlin can you dance in a crumbling old factory with a DIY bar.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they deflect complaints about BART delays with a joke about the Civic Center evacuation—it's a shared trauma out here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy serenade everyone on a packed 19 bus last night. It was like an impromptu concert. Only in San Francisco do silence and chaos blend so smoothly.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all local bars serve tapas, but many only offer pintxos. It's a whole different vibe when you know the ropes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their bewildered faces trying to figure out how to validate their tickets at the metro machines.
